item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sr. Researcher J. Harkness

Since when was Torchwood integrated into the Foundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand that the "other" in 3330-1's dialogue had to be changed due to circumstances, I just really really hate how it's replaced with 3125. I do share the headcanon that 3125 is connected to Fifthism (I actually consider it to be Fifthism itself), but I dislike whenever it's crosslinked in a Fifthist article.

I feel like SCP-3599 would work much better as a replacement; it fits the previously established connection with stars and while it may not necessarily be a hateful star, it's certainly a star with big plans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
when I saw a link in the -1 interview, and what words formed the link, I just KNEW it was going to be a crosslink to 3125. literally howled "i knew it!" out loud, confusing my partner who was sitting next to me at the time.

this should be more highly rated than it is imo. I really enjoyed it. I'm extremely passionate about music — I can literally babble on for hours about my favorite bands, they're my Special Interests™️ (some of them, anyway) — so, maybe because of that, I found this extra unsettling. an enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reverted an off-site link.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True. I just thought it'd be cool to read, but its still an awesome scp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was thinking about it, but the point is that they are so hard to complete, and some of them are hours long. Makes you wonder though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really wanting to find out more about the entity. Like, possibly have a "05 clearance" link that shows a log of the full musical scores and what happens when they ALL finish?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
from the MK wiki

Here's Johnny: Cage punches his opponent around, then forces his hands into the opponent's back, and proceeds to create a giant tear through the torso. He then peeks his head towards the opponent's open torso and says, "Hereeee's Johnny!" at the screen, then pulls back before the victim falls backward dead and bleeding profusely.

Huh, TIL

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, John Cage?

Didn't know you were such a fan. Here's Johnny! I don't know what just happened to me. I'd frame that. I make it look easy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my b, Site-59 is AROUND 2400

I misread, fixing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless I horribly misread SCP-2400, shouldn't it be Site-64T?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dammit, Cage.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Entries updated to be more Cage'y

i.e. had to be even weirder, dude was strange to begin with

EDIT: Also check out that little extra in the interview

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cage was strange, his thoughts were multifaceted. He also says

"There is no such thing as an empty space or an empty time. There is always something to see, something to hear. In fact, try as we may to make a silence, we cannot.

The highest purpose is to have no purpose at all. This puts one in accord with nature, in her manner of operation.
There is poetry as soon as we realize that we possess nothing.
We need not destroy the past. It is gone."

While I am currently revising the journal entries a bit to make them even more prosaic, the order of the events stands that his use of charts were only limited to those four performances. He did take a liking to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Definitely see it, yeah. "Beautiful Chaos" is a good way of putting it.

Listening to Australes where I came up with the idea for the items' cognitohazardous effects.

It was really strange, asynchronous and definitely not something I'd normally listen to. But I kept listening cause I could hear the talent still.

I kept it going longer than I thought I would, it perplexed me. I thought about him using both hands independently to not only make, but reprodouce that sound. I couldn't stop being an audience

He's got a mind that's made for this skip, too

"There is no such thing as an empty space or an empty time. There is always something to see, something to hear. In fact, try as we may to make a silence, we cannot.

The highest purpose is to have no purpose at all. This puts one in accord with nature, in her manner of operation.
There is poetry as soon as we realize that we possess nothing.
We need not destroy the past. It is gone."

Thoughts on me changing the plot to
[DATA EXPUNGED]
OK

Involve the progression of the pieces to use the third album, Freeman, as the focal point. Free man? That was the performance that would bring -1 right here, full form. It's a 3 hour performance.

Borealis would then be Cage's attempt to undo his mistakes, a performance that contained him and allowed him to live his normal life til his weird ass died four years later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm all for John Cage appearing on a mainlist article, but the journal entries sound strange, imagining them coming from Cage himself.

I think that any artistic product must stand or fall on what's there. A chimpanzee can do an abstract painting, if it's good, that's great! - John Cage

Love is memory. In the immediate present we don't love; life is too much with us. We lust, wilt, snort, swallow, gobble, hustle, nuzzle, etc. Later, memory flashes images swathed in nostalgia and yearning. We call that Love. Ha! Better to call it Madness. - John Cage

I love sounds. Just as they are. - John Cage

Obviously, this is an article about John Cage intercepted by a cult. Were you intending on telling a story about Cage developing a different perspective on sound, by looking at the stars?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is awesome.
John Cage's beautiful chaos and the Fifthist connection are a perfect fit for each other.

Also, I think I headcanon some nebulous connection to On Mount Golgotha.(no pun intended)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was more of a way of realizing the reasoning for his insanity, and the subtext about his fifthist friend pushing him in a certain direction.

I'm trying to think of more horror to add at the end, and I probably will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article got me reading about (and listening to; yeesh) the Etudes Australis, and all I can conclude is the man was insane. Nothing about this article convinces me that it's anything more than a straightforward application of that realization, and I just had a big shrug at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you! I wanted people to realize the scope of the church. And how much worse it could have been if his music were more mainstream.

One radio broadcast and…

Some of the things he says are quotes, and his music is actually quite weird imho

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that this ties 2040 and the Hateful Star into the Fifthist mythos in a huge way. I also appreciate the deepening of canon that this implies.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Second ever mainlist, first fifthist skip, hope i did them justice

I wanted to make this one similar but different to Star Signals.

Artist was not popular enough to cause a wide scale breach, but if they were, you'd have a 1425-level fuckup.

I also wanted to really expand the scope of the Fifthist universe, while still only implying whatever it is Fifthists worship. Basically pure chaotic destruction by a number of different skips

It's not as horrid as your typical fifth skip, but it still has their self keeping secrets and an implication of horror that extends beyond the scope of the writing.

Or at least, that's what I'm going for.

thanks yall!
